message:
🌱 Add keycloak: Persist session in JDBC store without using external mission (#868)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,72 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "keycloak-10803-persist-session-in-jdbc-store-without-using-external-infinispan-c",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "keycloak: Persist session in JDBC store without using external infinispan cluster",
+    "description": "Persist session in JDBC store without using external infinispan cluster. Requested by 33+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current Keycloak setup",
+        "description": "Verify your Keycloak version and current session persistence configuration:\n```bash\n# If running in a container:\ndocker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh show-config\n\n# Check if persistent-user-sessions feature is available:\ndocker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh build --features=persistent-user-sessions\n```\nThis feature is available in Keycloak 25+ and is enabled by default in Keycloak 26+."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Understand the problem",
+        "description": "By default, older Keycloak versions stored user sessions only in Infinispan caches. In a multi-node deployment, this required an external Infinispan cluster for session replication. Losing an Infinispan node could lead to session loss.\n\nThe persistent-user-sessions feature stores sessions in the database (JDBC), eliminating the need for an external Infinispan cluster while still maintaining session data across restarts."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Enable persistent user sessions",
+        "description": "Enable the persistent-user-sessions feature in Keycloak:\n```bash\n# Build Keycloak with the feature enabled:\n/opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh build --features=persistent-user-sessions\n\n# Or set it via environment variable:\nexport KC_FEATURES=persistent-user-sessions\n\n# Start Keycloak:\n/opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh start\n```\n\nIn Keycloak 26+, this feature is enabled by default and no additional configuration is needed. See the [source PR](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/15619) for implementation details."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify sessions persist across restarts",
+        "description": "Test that sessions survive a Keycloak server restart:\n1. Log in to Keycloak as a user to create an active session\n2. Check active sessions in the Admin Console under **Sessions**\n3. Restart the Keycloak container or pod\n4. Verify that the user session is still active after restart"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Keycloak added the persistent-user-sessions feature which stores user sessions in the database (JDBC store) instead of relying solely on Infinispan caches. This eliminates the need for an external Infinispan cluster in multi-node deployments. The feature is available in Keycloak 25+ and enabled by default in Keycloak 26+.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "keycloak",
+      "community",
+      "identity",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "keycloak"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "community",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/10803",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/15619"
+    },
+    "reactions": 33,
+    "comments": 8,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "tools": [
+      "docker"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Keycloak server (via Docker/Podman or Kubernetes with the Keycloak Operator) with database connectivity."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-12T06:48:40.055Z",
